rows.use_name_raw: coming into force date of this By-law Recycling depot use
Adult entertainment use C&D transfer, processing, and disposal use Heavy industrial use Obnoxious use Salvage use

footnotes.text: Use is permitted to occupy no more than 200 square metres of floor area per lot.
Use is permitted on a corner lot only.
Use is permitted within a mixed-use building that has no less than 75% of its floor area occupied by residential uses.
Use is permitted within the Dartmouth Waterfront (DW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3A, and the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B.

Use is permitted, except within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F. New or expanded residential uses within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, shall only be permitted by development agreement, in accordance with Section 497.
Use is not permitted, except for access and servicing functions to a lot or portion of a lot that permits the use.
Use is permitted accessory to a residential use provided that the dwelling unit is the primary residence of the operator, in accordance with Section 53. (RC-Feb 21/23;E-Sep 1/23)
Use is permitted, provided accessory office uses and accessory personal service uses must not exceed 25% of the total floor area of the building (RCCC-Dec10/25;E Dec31/25)

footnotes.text: Excluding heritage conservation districts, an existing building in the Young Avenue (YA) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3C, may undergo an internal conversion for no more than a three-unit dwelling use in compliance with the requirements of Section 63.
Use is permitted in accordance with the requirements of Section 54.

A three-unit dwelling use, four-unit dwelling use, or multi-unit dwelling use that contains up to 5 units is permitted in the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as shown on Schedule 3C. (RC-May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

Use is permitted, except within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F. New or expanded residential uses within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, shall only be permitted by development agreement, in accordance with Section 497.

A multi-unit dwelling use that contains up to 8 dwelling units is permitted in the ER-3 zone, in accordance with Section 231.3, and a multi-unit dwelling use that contains more than 8 units is permitted in the ER-3 zone in accordance with Section 63 or Subsection 233(3). (RC-May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

Within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as shown on Schedule 3C, a small shared housing use cannot be located in combination with a two-unit dwelling use, three-unit dwelling use, four-unit dwelling use, or a multi-unit dwelling use that contains up to 5 units. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)
Use is permitted accessory to a residential use provided that the dwelling unit is the primary residence of the operator, in accordance with Section 53. (RC-Feb 21/23;E-Sep 1/23)
A three-unit dwelling use, four-unit dwelling use, and multi-unit dwelling use are permitted in the ER-2 zone, in accordance with Section 63 or Subsection 233(3). (RC-May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

footnotes.text: Use is permitted if entirely contained within a building, excluding parking and loading facilities.

Retail use shall not exceed a maximum gross floor area of 300.0 square metres per premises. This requirement shall not apply to a building supply establishment or a retail use accessory to a permitted industrial use.
Use is permitted within the Mixed Industrial Commercial (MIC) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F.
Use is permitted on lands identified as a landmark building site on Schedule 9, as per the requirements of Section 43.
Use is permitted on the ground floor of a university or college building, and the floor area of the use cannot exceed 25% of the total floor area of the building.
Use is permitted in conjunction with another permitted main use.
Use is permitted accessory to a residential use provided that the dwelling unit is the primary residence of the operator, in accordance with Section 53. (RC-Feb 21/23;E-Sep 1/23)

Use is restricted to publicly owned or operated ferry terminals. (RC-Jan 9/24;E-Feb 9/24) (RC-Oct 7/25;E-Dec 3/25) (a) in the Northwest Arm Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, beyond the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12; and (RC-Oct 7/25;E-Dec 3/25) (b) in the Dartmouth Cove Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, beyond the reference line of Dartmouth Cove, as shown on Schedule 53. (RC-Oct 7/25;E-Dec 3/25)
Use is permitted in conjunction with another permitted main institutional use except on the lands that is identified as a landmark building site on Schedule 9. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)
Use is permitted, including on lands identified as a landmark building site on Schedule 9 subject to the requirements of Section 43. (RCCC-Dec10/25;E Dec31/25)

footnotes.text: In the Morris Queen (MQ) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, a four-unit dwelling use is permitted.
Use is permitted in accordance with the requirements of Section 54. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)
Use is permitted within a Schmidtville Heritage Building that existed on July 17, 2018 and is identified on Schedule 23.
A four-unit dwelling use is permitted within a Schmidtville Heritage Building that existed on July 17, 2018 and is identified on Schedule 23. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

Multi-unit dwelling use containing 5 to 10 units is permitted within a Schmidtville Heritage Building that existed on July 17, 2018 and is located on a lot identified on Schedule 23 as allowing 5 to 10 units.

Use is permitted within a Schmidtville Heritage Building that existed on July 17, 2018 and is located on a lot identified on Schedule 23 as allowing restaurant uses and local commercial uses in addition to all other uses permitted in the HCD-SV Zone.
Use is permitted in the Wright Avenue (WA) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F.
Use is permitted accessory to a residential use provided that the dwelling unit is the primary residence of the operator, in accordance with Section 53. (RC-Feb 21/23;E-Sep 1/23
